properties.name: Kathmandu Durbar Square
properties.description: The historic plaza in front of the ancient royal palace of the former Kathmandu Kingdom. One of three UNESCO World Heritage Durbar Squares in the Kathmandu Valley.

properties.name: Patan Durbar Square
properties.description: Situated in the center of Lalitpur city, this UNESCO Heritage Site displays the finest Newari architecture with the artistic Patan Museum.

properties.name: Bhaktapur Durbar Square
properties.description: An ancient palace complex with four distinct squares. Famous for the 55-Window Palace, Nyatapola Temple, and exquisite pottery craftsmanship.

properties.name: Swayambhunath Stupa (Monkey Temple)
properties.description: An ancient religious complex atop a hill in the Kathmandu Valley, sacred to both Buddhists and Hindus with panoramic views of the city.

properties.name: Pashupatinath Temple
properties.description: One of the most sacred Hindu temples of Lord Shiva, located on the banks of the holy Bagmati River with a vast complex of temples, ashrams, and inscriptions.

properties.name: Boudhanath Stupa
properties.description: One of the largest spherical stupas in Nepal and the holiest Tibetan Buddhist temple outside Tibet. A center of Tibetan culture in Kathmandu.

properties.name: Patan Museum
properties.description: Nepal's finest museum, situated within the Royal Palace of Patan Durbar Square. Houses an exceptional collection of religious and historical artifacts.

properties.name: Chandragiri Hills
properties.description: A scenic hill southwest of Kathmandu at 2551m altitude with a cable car, offering stunning views of the Himalayan range from Dhaulagiri to Everest.

properties.name: Godavari Botanical Garden
properties.description: National Botanical Garden of Nepal spanning 82 hectares at the foot of Phulchoki Hill, featuring diverse flora from tropical to alpine zones.

properties.name: Nagarkot Viewpoint
properties.description: A hill station east of Kathmandu famous for some of the finest Himalayan panoramic views, especially beautiful during sunrise and sunset.

properties.name: Kakani Viewpoint
properties.description: A serene hill station north of Kathmandu at 2073m offering panoramic views of Ganesh Himal, Langtang, and other Himalayan peaks.

properties.name: Namo Buddha Monastery
properties.description: A sacred hilltop stupa and Tibetan Buddhist monastery east of Kathmandu. One of the most important pilgrimage sites in Nepal.

properties.name: Shivapuri Nagarjun National Park
properties.description: A protected area covering 159 sq km on the northern rim of Kathmandu valley. Excellent birdwatching, hiking, and mountain biking destination.

properties.name: Rani Pokhari (Queen's Pond)
properties.description: A historic artificial pond built in 1670 AD by King Pratap Malla in memory of his queen. Features a small Shiva temple on an island in the center.

properties.name: Lumbini — Birthplace of Buddha
properties.description: UNESCO World Heritage Site — the birthplace of Siddhartha Gautama (Buddha) in 563 BC. Features the sacred Mayadevi Temple, Ashoka Pillar, and a sacred garden.

properties.name: Mayadevi Temple
properties.description: The central shrine in the sacred garden of Lumbini, built over the exact spot where Queen Mayadevi gave birth to Siddhartha Gautama.

properties.name: Tansen (Palpa) Historic Town
properties.description: A charming hilltop town known for its Newari heritage, traditional dhaka fabric weaving, historic Tansen Durbar, and panoramic mountain views.

properties.name: Kapilvastu Museum
properties.description: Museum near the ancient Kapilvastu ruins, the kingdom where Prince Siddhartha grew up. Displays significant archaeological artifacts from excavations.

properties.name: Bandevi Devi Temple & Forest
properties.description: A forested hilltop temple dedicated to Bandevi Devi, a local female deity. Surrounded by dense forest and frequented by pilgrims and nature lovers alike.

properties.name: Siwaliks Ridge Trail, Pyuthan
properties.description: An underexplored trekking destination featuring sub-Himalayan Siwalik hills with dense forests, waterfalls, and excellent bird diversity.

properties.name: Phewa Lake
properties.description: A beautiful freshwater lake in Pokhara, famous for the reflection of Mount Machhapuchhre and the Tal Barahi Temple situated on an island.

properties.name: Sarangkot Viewpoint
properties.description: A popular hilltop viewpoint at 1592m near Pokhara offering spectacular sunrise views of the Annapurna Range and Pokhara Valley.

properties.name: Annapurna Base Camp
properties.description: A premier trekking destination situated at 4130m surrounded by a 360° amphitheater of Himalayan peaks including Annapurna I (8091m).

properties.name: Poon Hill
properties.description: A celebrated viewpoint at 3210m on the Ghorepani-Poon Hill Circuit, offering one of the most accessible and spectacular sunrise panoramas of the Annapurna and Dhaulagiri ranges.

properties.name: World Peace Pagoda, Pokhara
properties.description: A gleaming white Buddhist Shanti Stupa (Peace Pagoda) atop a hill south of Phewa Lake, offering panoramic views of Pokhara, the lake, and Himalayan peaks.

properties.name: Devi's Falls (Patale Chhango)
properties.description: A unique waterfall in Pokhara where the Phusre Khola stream disappears underground into a deep gorge. Particularly spectacular during the monsoon season.

properties.name: Ghorepani Village
properties.description: A classic trekking waypoint on the Annapurna Circuit at 2874m, a hub for the Poon Hill trek, surrounded by rhododendron forests blooming brilliantly in spring.

properties.name: Lo Manthang, Upper Mustang
properties.description: The walled medieval city that served as the capital of the ancient Kingdom of Lo (Mustang). A restricted trekking area preserving unique Tibetan-influenced culture and cave monasteries.

properties.name: Muktinath Temple
properties.description: A sacred pilgrimage site at 3710m considered holy by both Hindus and Buddhists. Famous for the 108 bull-headed spouts and natural flames amid water.

properties.name: Manang Village
properties.description: A classic rest point on the Annapurna Circuit at 3519m. A traditional high-altitude Tibetan village with stunning views of Annapurna, Gangapurna, and Pisang Peak.

properties.name: Gorkha Durbar Palace
properties.description: The ancestral home of the Shah dynasty and birthplace of King Prithvi Narayan Shah, the founder of the modern state of Nepal, sitting atop a steep hill.

properties.name: Mount Everest Base Camp
properties.description: The world-famous trekking destination at 5364m, sitting at the foot of the world's tallest mountain (8848.86m). An iconic journey through Sherpa culture and high-altitude wilderness.

properties.name: Khumbu Icefall
properties.description: A dramatic and dangerous glacier at the head of the Khumbu Valley on the route to Mount Everest. One of the world's most awe-inspiring ice formations.

properties.name: Namche Bazaar
properties.description: The bustling gateway to the Khumbu region at 3440m — a vibrant market town and acclimatization stop for Everest trekkers with a fascinating Sherpa museum.

properties.name: Tengboche Monastery
properties.description: The most famous Buddhist monastery in the Khumbu region, perched at 3867m with breathtaking views of Ama Dablam and Everest. A spiritual landmark for Sherpa culture.

properties.name: Kanchenjunga Base Camp
properties.description: Base camp at the foot of the world's third highest mountain (8586m). A remote and pristine trek through rhododendron forests, glaciers, and diverse wildlife.

properties.name: Ilam Tea Gardens
properties.description: Picturesque rolling hills covered with lush green tea estates in eastern Nepal, producing some of the finest artisanal teas. A serene retreat with walking trails through the gardens.

properties.name: Mechi River Corridor
properties.description: The Mechi River valley bordering India forming a lush corridor of biodiversity with forests, riverine habitats, and excellent birdwatching opportunities.

properties.name: Koshi Tappu Wildlife Reserve
properties.description: Nepal's smallest wildlife reserve, a Ramsar wetland site and critical habitat for migratory waterfowl, the last wild water buffalo in Nepal, and gharial crocodiles.

properties.name: Halesi Mahadev Cave Temple
properties.description: A sacred cave temple complex considered one of the holiest Hindu pilgrimage sites in Nepal, believed to be where Lord Shiva once meditated.

properties.name: Parsa Wildlife Reserve
properties.description: A wildlife reserve bordering Chitwan National Park, serving as a buffer zone protecting Bengal tigers, elephants, and diverse birdlife in the Terai ecosystem.

properties.name: Manakamana Temple
properties.description: A Nepalese Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Manakamana, perched atop a hill at 1302m. Accessed by one of Asia's highest cable car rides.

properties.name: Tsum Valley
properties.description: A deeply remote sacred Himalayan valley offering pristine Buddhist culture, ancient monasteries, and stunning mountain scenery. A hidden gem accessible only to trekkers.

properties.name: Rara Lake National Park
properties.description: Nepal's largest and most pristine lake at 2990m in remote Karnali province. Renowned for its crystal-clear azure waters, alpine meadows, and surrounding forest.

properties.name: Shey Phoksundo Lake
properties.description: The deepest and most beautiful lake in Nepal at 3611m altitude, with hauntingly blue-green waters. Nepal's largest national park surrounds it with diverse wildlife.

properties.name: Rara Lake Trekking Trail
properties.description: One of Nepal's most remote and rewarding trekking routes passing through diverse landscapes, traditional villages, and culminating at the stunning Rara Lake.

properties.name: Karnali River Rafting
properties.description: One of the world's top whitewater rafting rivers, offering multi-day expedition through dramatic gorges, rapids, and some of the most pristine scenery in Nepal.

properties.name: Simikot, Humla District
properties.description: The remote headquarters of Humla district and gateway to Tibet's Mount Kailash. A hub for trekking and cultural exploration in Nepal's most isolated region.

properties.name: Api Himal Base Camp
properties.description: Base camp approach to Api Himal (7132m), the highest peak of the Mahabharat Range. An extremely remote and challenging trekking route for serious mountaineers.

properties.name: Shuklaphanta National Park
properties.description: A wildlife reserve in the Terai grasslands supporting Bengal tigers, swamp deer, and elephants. Home to the world's largest congregation of swamp deer.

properties.name: Mahakali River Adventure Zone
properties.description: The Mahakali River forms Nepal's western border with India, offering exciting rafting opportunities through scenic gorges in one of Nepal's remotest regions.

properties.name: Dhangadhi Gateway & Terai Culture
properties.description: The main city of far-western Nepal and gateway to Terai's cultural richness, Tharu community traditions, and access to Shuklaphanta reserve.

properties.name: Bedkot Lake
properties.description: A tranquil natural lake with dense surrounding forests providing habitat to migratory birds and diverse wildlife in far-western Nepal.

properties.name: Butwal & Siddhartha Highway
properties.description: The commercial gateway city along the Siddhartha Highway connecting the Terai with Pokhara. Known for its surrounding hills and its role as a crossroads of cultures.

properties.name: Bardia National Park
properties.description: Nepal's largest national park in the Terai, known for exceptional chances of seeing wild Bengal tigers in natural behavior, along with elephants, rhinos, and dolphins.

properties.name: Birendranagar & Bulbule Lake
properties.description: The provincial capital of Karnali surrounded by hills, Bulbule Lake is a serene local attraction with boating and birdwatching opportunities.

properties.name: Thorong La Pass
properties.description: The highest point on the world-famous Annapurna Circuit at 5416m. A challenging high-altitude mountain pass with breathtaking views of the entire Annapurna Range.

properties.name: Kagbeni Medieval Village
properties.description: A medieval walled village at the confluence of the Kali Gandaki and Jhong rivers, marking the checkpoint for the restricted Upper Mustang region with ancient monasteries.

properties.name: Ridi Temple & Gorge
properties.description: A revered Hindu pilgrimage site at the confluence of Ridi Khola and Kali Gandaki River, featuring a historic Vishnu temple and a dramatic rocky gorge.

properties.name: Makalu Base Camp
properties.description: Base camp for the fifth highest mountain in the world (8485m). The Makalu-Barun National Park trail passes through some of Nepal's most diverse and remote wilderness.

properties.name: Arun Valley Trekking
properties.description: The Arun River valley in eastern Nepal is one of the world's deepest valleys, offering stunning vertical landscapes from tropical jungle to high alpine zones.

properties.name: Khumbu Valley
properties.description: The famed Khumbu region in northeastern Nepal — the homeland of the Sherpa people, world's most extraordinary mountain scenery including four 8000m peaks.

properties.name: Yartung Festival, Lo Manthang
properties.description: The annual Yartung festival in Upper Mustang is one of Nepal's most spectacular cultural events, featuring horse racing, dances, and rituals unique to Tibetan Buddhism.

properties.name: Gosainkunda Lake
properties.description: A sacred alpine lake at 4380m in Langtang National Park, considered holy by both Hindus and Buddhists. Site of the famous Janai Purnima pilgrimage during August.

properties.name: Helambu Trekking Region
properties.description: A popular lower-altitude trekking area north of Kathmandu offering panoramic mountain views, traditional Hyolmo and Sherpa villages, and Buddhist monasteries.

properties.name: Tharu Cultural Museum & Resort
properties.description: A fascinating museum celebrating the unique Tharu indigenous culture of the Terai lowlands — their crafts, traditional housing, fishing traditions, and celebrations.

properties.name: Sauraha Elephant Breeding Center
properties.description: A government elephant breeding and training center in Sauraha near Chitwan, where visitors can observe these magnificent animals, their calves, and their daily care routines.

properties.name: Gupteshwar Mahadev Cave
properties.description: A sacred Hindu cave temple near Pokhara dedicated to Lord Shiva. The cave contains a natural stalagmite worshipped as Shivalinga and connects to a lower gorge area.

properties.name: International Mountain Museum, Pokhara
properties.description: One of the finest mountain museums in the world, housing exhibits on the geology, ecology, culture, and history of the Himalayas and the heroic stories of mountaineers.

properties.name: Bindhyabasini Temple, Pokhara
properties.description: One of the most important Hindu temples in Pokhara, dedicated to Goddess Bhagwati. Situated on a hilltop with commanding views of the Annapurna range and the city.

properties.name: Begnas Lake
properties.description: The second-largest lake in the Pokhara Valley and a much quieter alternative to Phewa Lake. Excellent for boating, fishing, and enjoying the surrounding green hills.

properties.name: Ramdi River Canyon
properties.description: A dramatic river canyon popular for bungee jumping and extreme activities. The Kali Gandaki river creates stunning geological formations in this accessible canyon.

properties.name: Phulchoki Hill
properties.description: The highest point surrounding the Kathmandu Valley at 2782m, covered in dense temperate forests excellent for birdwatching, and offering outstanding Himalayan views.

properties.name: Dakshinkali Temple
properties.description: A major Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Kali, located in a forested gorge at the southwestern end of Kathmandu Valley. Famous for animal sacrifices during festivals.

properties.name: Balthali Eco Village
properties.description: A peaceful rural retreat in the hills east of Kathmandu with community-based eco-tourism offering organic farming, mountain views, hiking trails, and authentic village life.

properties.name: Tribhuvan Museum
properties.description: Dedicated to King Tribhuvan who overthrew the Rana oligarchy and restored democracy to Nepal in 1951. The museum houses royal artifacts and significant historical exhibits.

properties.name: Gokyo Lakes
properties.description: A chain of six high-altitude freshwater lakes in the Khumbu region at 4700-5000m. The Gokyo Ri viewpoint above offers one of the finest Himalayan panoramas.

properties.name: Phaplu & Solu Valley
properties.description: A beautiful Sherpa valley south of Everest region with the historic Chiwong Monastery and Treksure Gompa. Lower altitude alternative to the main Khumbu treks.

properties.name: Hetauda Industrial Heritage
properties.description: Nepal's first industrial city in the Terai foothills. Known for its ropeway system (the world's second longest when built) and growing industrial heritage.

properties.name: Mai Pokhari Lake
properties.description: A sacred high-altitude lake on the ridge bordering India in eastern Nepal, known for its scenic beauty, surrounding forest, and religious significance.

properties.name: Budhabare & Eastern Terai Wetlands
properties.description: Rich lowland wetlands and forests of the eastern Terai region important for migratory bird conservation and providing habitat to numerous endangered species.

properties.name: Pokhara Paragliding Zone
properties.description: Pokhara is Asia's premier paragliding destination. Tandem flights from Sarangkot launch site soar over Phewa Lake and across to the stunning Annapurna panorama.

properties.name: Kopan Monastery
properties.description: A Tibetan Buddhist monastery on a hilltop north of Kathmandu offering meditation courses, retreats, and teachings in Tibetan Buddhism. Known worldwide for its month-long courses.

properties.name: Budhanilkantha Temple
properties.description: Features a remarkable 5-meter stone statue of Vishnu reclining on the Shesha Naag (thousand-headed serpent) in the middle of a sacred pond. A major Hindu site.

properties.name: Lumle Agricultural Research Center
properties.description: A hill agricultural research station set in beautiful terraced landscape with one of Nepal's highest annual rainfalls, featuring demonstration gardens and mountain views.

properties.name: Machhapuchhre Base Camp
properties.description: Base camp at 3700m for the revered 'Fishtail' peak (6993m), considered sacred and never permitted to be climbed. A stunning viewpoint on the Annapurna Sanctuary trek.

properties.name: Juphal Airport & Dolpo Gateway
properties.description: Juphal serves as the main entry point to Dolpo — one of the most remote and culturally preserved regions of Nepal, featuring ancient Bon culture and dramatic landscapes.

properties.name: Nepalgunj Cultural Bazaar
properties.description: A vibrant trading city on the Terai with a unique blend of Nepali, Muslim, and Indian cultures. A major hub for flights to remote western Nepal destinations.

properties.name: Dang Valley Cultural Area
properties.description: Nepal's largest inner Terai valley surrounded by forested hills, home to the Tharu and Dangaura communities with rich folklore, traditional dances, and cultural practices.

properties.name: Marsyangdi River Rafting
properties.description: One of Nepal's best whitewater rafting rivers with exciting Grade IV rapids flowing through the dramatic Annapurna foothills gorge. Multi-day runs available.

properties.name: Gorkha Panoramic Viewpoint
properties.description: An elevated viewpoint in the Gorkha district offering stunning panoramic views of Manaslu, Ganesh Himal, Sringi Himal, and the foothills of mid-western Nepal.

properties.name: Mai Valley Trekking
properties.description: A scenic trekking route through the lush hills and valleys of Ilam district, passing through tea gardens, rhododendron forests, and traditional farming villages.

properties.name: Thubten Choling Monastery, Junbesi
properties.description: A large and active Nyingma Tibetan Buddhist monastery in the Solu region, housing hundreds of monks and nuns, a popular pilgrimage and cultural tourism stop.

properties.name: Mahendra Cave & Bat Cave
properties.description: An intriguing natural limestone cave system near Pokhara featuring stalactites and stalagmites and a colony of thousands of bats that emerge dramatically at dusk.

properties.name: Panchkhal Valley
properties.description: A fertile river valley east of Kathmandu with a warm microclimate supporting diverse agriculture, orange orchards, and serving as a gateway to eastern trekking regions.

properties.name: Kakrebihar Archaeological Site
properties.description: An important archaeological site featuring ancient Buddhist and Hindu temple ruins from the Licchavi period. One of mid-western Nepal's most significant heritage sites.

properties.name: Bansbari Forest Reserve
properties.description: A community forest reserve near the Indian border in far-western Nepal, providing habitat to tigers and diverse mammals in the last extension of the Terai arc landscape.

properties.name: Jagadishpur Reservoir
properties.description: An important Ramsar wetland site near Lumbini that attracts huge flocks of migratory waterfowl including over 150 bird species and 20,000+ individual birds in winter.

properties.name: World Peace Pagoda (Shanti Stupa), Lumbini
properties.description: A gleaming Japanese-built Peace Pagoda in the sacred garden of Lumbini, dedicated to world peace through the teachings of the Buddha. A striking white landmark.

properties.name: Syangja Walling Bazaar & Hills
properties.description: A mid-hills district with scenic terraced hillsides, traditional Gurung villages, and beautiful viewpoints towards the Annapurna and Dhaulagiri ranges.

properties.name: Nar Phu Valley (Restricted)
properties.description: A restricted valley off the Annapurna Circuit with pristine Tibetan Buddhist culture and dramatic high-altitude landscapes. One of Nepal's most authentic trekking destinations.

properties.name: Ganesh Himal Base Camp Trek
properties.description: A challenging and rewarding trek to the base camp of Ganesh Himal (7422m), one of Nepal's lesser-known mountain giants with stunning high-altitude wilderness.

properties.name: Island Peak (Imja Tse) Climbing
properties.description: A popular trekking peak in the Khumbu region at 6189m, often used as training for higher Himalayan climbs. Offers stunning close-up views of Lhotse and the Amphu Lapcha.

properties.name: Ama Dablam Base Camp
properties.description: Base camp for Ama Dablam (6812m), widely considered one of the world's most beautiful mountains with its distinctive twin 'arms'. A revered mountaineering objective.

properties.name: Jiri — Classic Everest Trek Start
properties.description: The original starting point for the classic Everest Base Camp trek, before the Lukla airport was built. Offers a challenging and authentic trekking experience through mid-hills villages.

properties.name: Tenzing-Hillary Airport (Lukla)
properties.description: The world-famous gateway to the Everest region, often listed among the world's most dramatic airports. Landing here begins the iconic Everest Base Camp trek.

properties.name: Kirtipur Ancient City
properties.description: One of the oldest settlements in the Kathmandu Valley, perched atop a hill with beautifully preserved Newari architecture, old courtyards, and a fascinating history of resistance.

properties.name: Garden of Dreams (Kaiser Mahal)
properties.description: A neoclassical heritage garden in the heart of Kathmandu, restored to its original 1920s glory with pavilions, fountains, ponds, and lush landscapes — a serene urban oasis.

properties.name: Besisahar — Annapurna Circuit Start
properties.description: The traditional starting point of the world-famous Annapurna Circuit trek, located at 760m in Lamjung district along the Marsyangdi River.

properties.name: Seti River Gorge, Pokhara
properties.description: An unusual and captivating gorge in the middle of Pokhara city where the milky Seti River runs invisibly underground through a dramatic narrow slot canyon.

properties.name: Hiranya Varna Mahavihar (Golden Temple)
properties.description: A magnificent Buddhist monastery in the heart of Patan dating back to the 12th century, famous for its spectacular golden facade and intricate metal and woodwork.

properties.name: Nagarjun Forest Reserve
properties.description: A lush forest reserve on the northwestern edge of Kathmandu Valley offering easy day hikes, mountain biking, wildlife spotting, and peaceful escape from the city.

properties.name: Larcha Valley, Sindhupalchok
properties.description: A pristine valley near the Tibet border accessed from Tatopani hot springs, featuring beautiful alpine landscapes, yak pastures, and connections to the historic Tibet trade routes.

properties.name: Gaurishankar Conservation Area
properties.description: A protected conservation area centered on the sacred mountain Gaurishankar (7134m) with diverse fauna including red pandas, snow leopards, and Himalayan black bears.

properties.name: Jajarkot Fort & Hills
properties.description: The headquarters of Jajarkot district features an ancient hilltop fort overlooking the Tila River valley with important historical connections to medieval Nepal.

properties.name: Sinja Valley Archaeological Site
properties.description: The historic birthplace of the Nepali language, once the capital of the powerful medieval Khasa Kingdom. A UNESCO tentative list site with significant archaeological remains.

properties.name: Chandannath Temple, Jumla
properties.description: A significant ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Chandannath, located in Jumla district with spectacular surrounding mountains and serving as an important pilgrimage destination.

properties.name: Sun Koshi River Rafting
properties.description: One of the top 10 river journeys in the world, the 8-10 day Sun Koshi expedition traverses 270km through pristine gorges, remote villages, and exciting whitewater.

properties.name: Suryodaya Viewpoint
properties.description: An outstanding dawn viewpoint in the Ilam hills offering panoramic views of the entire eastern Himalayan range including Kanchenjunga (8586m) and surrounding peaks.

properties.name: Tatopani Hot Springs
properties.description: Natural thermal hot springs on the banks of the Bhote Koshi River, popular for trekkers returning from Tibet or the Langtang region, providing soothing relief for tired muscles.

properties.name: Tatopani Hot Springs (Kali Gandaki)
properties.description: Famous hot springs on the Annapurna Circuit route along the Kali Gandaki gorge where trekkers traditionally rest and bathe after the Thorong La pass crossing.

properties.name: Lamjung Durbar Museum
properties.description: A restored royal palace of the Lamjung Kingdom, showcasing the history of this mid-hills principality and its contributions to the unification of Nepal.

properties.name: Daman Viewpoint
properties.description: At 2322m on the Tribhuvan Highway, Daman offers one of the most sweeping panoramas of the Himalayas — from Dhaulagiri to Everest, a 340km stretch of peaks.

properties.name: Davis Falls (Begnas to Pokhara Corridor)
properties.description: The scenic corridor between Begnas Lake and Pokhara city offering beautiful terraced rice fields, community forests, and idyllic rural landscapes with mountain backdrops.

properties.name: Damak & Himalayan Refugees Cultural Center
properties.description: Damak in the eastern Terai is home to a large Tibetan and Bhutanese refugee community, offering unique cultural experiences, monasteries, and traditional crafts.

properties.name: Last Resort Bungee & Canyon
properties.description: Nepal's highest and most thrilling bungee jump from a suspension bridge over the roaring Bhote Koshi River (160m drop). Also features canyon swinging and zip-lining.

properties.name: Manma & Kalikot Cultural Heritage
properties.description: The headquarters of Kalikot district features ancient stone houses, traditional mid-western culture, and views of the Karnali River gorge in a rarely visited setting.

properties.name: Babai River Wilderness (Bardia)
properties.description: The remote Babai Valley within Bardia National Park offers exceptional wilderness experiences with extremely high chances of tiger sightings away from tourist crowds.

properties.name: Dhulikhel Heritage Town
properties.description: A historic hilltop town east of Kathmandu with beautifully preserved traditional architecture, excellent panoramic mountain views, and a thriving local art scene.

properties.name: Pumdi Bhumdi Eco Resort Area
properties.description: A serene community forest and eco-tourism destination near Pokhara providing habitat for wildlife while offering peaceful nature walks, bird watching, and authentic rural experiences.

properties.name: Gadhimai Temple
properties.description: A famous Hindu temple known for the Gadhimai festival held every five years.

properties.name: Chhinnamasta Bhagawati
properties.description: A renowned Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Chhinnamasta, highly revered in the eastern Terai.

properties.name: Jaleshwor Mahadev
properties.description: A historic Shiva temple uniquely situated in water, an important pilgrimage site.

properties.name: Dhanushadham
properties.description: A mythological site related to the Ramayana, where a piece of Shiva’s bow is believed to have fallen.

properties.name: Sahlesh Phulbari
properties.description: A sacred forest and garden with a unique orchid that blooms on the first day of the Nepali New Year.

properties.name: Bhedia Lake
properties.description: A serene wetland ecosystem in the Terai, excellent for local bird watching and boating.

properties.name: Routa Pokhari
properties.description: A scenic hilltop pond surrounded by dense forest, offering a tranquil retreat and religious significance.

properties.name: Badimalika Temple
properties.description: A major pilgrimage site located at a high altitude surrounded by spectacular rolling meadows.

properties.name: Ramaroshan Area
properties.description: A stunning complex of 12 lakes and 18 meadows, often called the "Pancha Puri" of the far west.

properties.name: Ghodaghodi Lake
properties.description: A Ramsar site wetland of immense ecological value, home to crocodiles, turtles, and rare birds.

properties.name: Tikapur Park
properties.description: One of Nepal’s largest and most beautiful botanical gardens, built by King Mahendra.

properties.name: Karnali Bridge (Chisapani)
properties.description: An iconic asymmetric, single-tower cable-stayed bridge crossing the mighty Karnali River.

properties.name: Shaileshwari Temple
properties.description: A prominent Hindu temple in Dipayal, highly revered as one of the major Shakti Peeths in the far-west.

properties.name: Surma Sarovar
properties.description: A high-altitude holy lake renowned for its pristine alpine beauty and religious importance.

properties.name: Kakre Bihar
properties.description: Intricate ruins of a 12th-century stone temple reflecting both Hindu and Buddhist architectural styles.

properties.name: Bulbule Taal
properties.description: A natural spring-fed lake set within a peaceful park in Birendranagar.

properties.name: Limi Valley
properties.description: A highly remote, culturally rich valley offering off-the-beaten-path trekking near the Tibetan border.

properties.name: Khalidada Viewpoint
properties.description: A scenic ridge offering unparalleled views of Rara Lake and the surrounding Himalayan peaks.

properties.name: Dullu Heritage Area
properties.description: Historical site containing the earliest known Nepali inscription (Damupal's inscription) and ancient stone pillars.

properties.name: Baraha Kshetra
properties.description: One of Nepal’s four major pilgrimage sites, situated at the confluence of the Koshi and Koka rivers.

properties.name: Namche Bazaar
properties.description: The famous "Gateway to Everest," a bustling Sherpa town nestled in a horseshoe-shaped basin.

properties.name: Gokyo Lakes
properties.description: A series of breathtaking oligotrophic lakes at 4,700m, offering one of the best Everest region panoramas.

properties.name: Bhedetar Viewpoint
properties.description: A popular hill station providing sweeping views of the plains and the Makalu Himalayan range.

properties.name: Milke Danda
properties.description: A high ridge famously known as the capital of rhododendrons, blooming spectacularly in spring.

properties.name: Mai Pokhari
properties.description: A star-shaped lake surrounded by pristine forest; a Ramsar wetland site sacred to both Hindus and Buddhists.

properties.name: Halesi Mahadev
properties.description: A spectacular natural cave system revered as the "Pashupatinath of the East" by Hindus and Buddhists.

properties.name: Mardi Himal Trek
properties.description: A stunning, relatively new ridge trek offering incredibly close views of Machhapuchhre (Fishtail) and Annapurna.

properties.name: Kagbeni Village
properties.description: A fascinating medieval-looking village with mud-packed houses, acting as the gateway to Upper Mustang.

properties.name: Tsho Rolpa Glacial Lake
properties.description: One of the largest glacial lakes in Nepal, situated in the dramatic Rolwaling Valley at 4,580m.

properties.name: Taudaha Lake
properties.description: A tranquil lake in the Kathmandu Valley, historically believed to house the serpent king Karkotak.

properties.name: Shivapuri Nagarjun NP
properties.description: The closest national park to Kathmandu, offering excellent day hikes, watershed protection, and rich biodiversity.

properties.name: Dakshinkali Temple
properties.description: A highly revered shrine dedicated to Goddess Kali, located in a forested gorge south of Kathmandu.

properties.name: Tilaurakot (Ancient Kapilavastu)
properties.description: The ancient capital city where Siddhartha Gautama (Lord Buddha) spent the first 29 years of his life.

properties.name: Jagadishpur Reservoir
properties.description: Nepal’s largest man-made lake and a crucial Ramsar wetland site for migratory birds.

properties.name: Ruru Kshetra (Ridi)
properties.description: A sacred Hindu pilgrimage site at the confluence of the Kali Gandaki and Ridi rivers.

properties.name: Phoksundo Lake
properties.description: Nepal’s deepest lake, famous for its mesmerizing turquoise color, located in Shey Phoksundo National Park.

properties.name: Langtang Valley Trek
properties.description: The "Valley of Glaciers", offering incredible alpine scenery and rich Tamang culture near the Tibetan border.

properties.name: Chitlang Village
properties.description: An ancient Newari settlement on the old trade route, now famous for homestays and organic farming.

properties.name: Gorkha Durbar
properties.description: The historic palace of King Prithvi Narayan Shah, perched dramatically on a fortified hilltop.

properties.name: Manaslu Circuit
properties.description: An epic, challenging tea-house trek circling the world’s 8th highest peak, crossing the Larkya La pass.

properties.name: Koshi Tappu Wildlife Reserve
properties.description: A premier bird-watching destination and the last habitat of the wild water buffalo in Nepal.

properties.name: Kanchenjunga Base Camp
properties.description: A demanding and pristine trek to the base of the world’s 3rd highest mountain.

properties.name: Dhampus Village
properties.description: A beautiful Gurung village offering classic Annapurna range sunrises without rigorous trekking.

properties.name: Balthali Eco-Village
properties.description: A peaceful, off-the-beaten-path village retreat with terraced farms, orange orchards, and suspension bridges.

properties.name: Dhorpatan Hunting Reserve
properties.description: Nepal’s only hunting reserve, famous for blue sheep and stunning high-altitude marshlands.

properties.name: Doleshwor Mahadev
properties.description: Believed to be the missing head of the Kedarnath Jyotirlinga, a significant Shiva temple.

properties.name: Palanchowk Bhagawati
properties.description: An intricately carved 5th-century stone idol of Goddess Bhagawati atop a scenic hill.

properties.name: Nar Phu Valley Trek
properties.description: A restricted area trek offering raw Tibetan culture, ancient monasteries, and rugged landscapes.

properties.name: Tansen, Palpa
properties.description: A historic hill town renowned for Dhaka fabric, Newari architecture, and the Srinagar Danda viewpoint.

properties.name: Rani Mahal
properties.description: Known as the "Taj Mahal of Nepal", a stunning palace built on the banks of the Kali Gandaki river.

properties.name: Supa Deurali Temple
properties.description: A famous Hindu temple situated dramatically in a mountain gorge, surrounded by local legends.

properties.name: Swargadwari
properties.description: A major hilltop pilgrimage site famous for its continuous Vedic fire (Akhanda Yagya) and ashram.

properties.name: Khaptad National Park
properties.description: A unique rolling plateau featuring moorland, steep slopes, and an ashram of the Khaptad Baba.

properties.name: Shuklaphanta National Park
properties.description: Home to the largest herd of swamp deer in the world and exceptional tiger habitats in the Terai.

properties.name: Dodhara Chandani Bridge
properties.description: One of the longest multi-span suspension bridges in Asia, crossing the massive Mahakali river.

properties.name: Bajhang Durbar
properties.description: Ruins and remnants of the ancient Malla kings’ palace offering historical insight into the far-west.

properties.name: Api Nampa Conservation Area
properties.description: A pristine, remote protected area featuring Mount Api and rich high-Himalayan biodiversity.

properties.name: Panch Pokhari
properties.description: A group of five holy lakes at 4,100m, offering incredible views of the Jugal Himal range.

properties.name: Siddha Gufa
properties.description: The largest cave in Nepal, spanning nearly 500 meters deep with dramatic stalactite formations.

properties.name: Gufa Pokhari
properties.description: A quiet, reflective lake on the Milke Danda ridge offering stunning vistas of Makalu and Kanchenjunga.

properties.name: Chandragiri Hills
properties.description: A major viewpoint accessed by cable car, offering sweeping panoramas of the Kathmandu Valley and Himalayas.
